name: ANKYLOSING SPONDYLITIS
definition: autoimmune disease of unknown etiology characterized by inflammation of multiple articular

demographics.Frequency: 1% of patients with ankylosing spondylitis, usually at an advanced stage of
demographics.Histo: interstitial + pleural fibrosis with foci of dense collagen deposition, NO granulomas

MR: arthritis of zygapophyseal (facet), costovertebral, costotransverse joints (best seen on
ankylosis in late stage → impairing chest excursion
enthesitis of interspinous ligaments ± osteitis of subjacent spinous processes
Skull: temporomandibular joint space narrowing, erosions, osteophytosis
Hand: osteoporosis, joint space narrowing, osseous erosions (deformities less striking than in
Sacroiliac joint / symphysis pubis: initially sclerosis of joint margins primarily on iliac side (bilateral + symmetric late in
later irregularities + widening of joint (= cartilage destruction)
Pelvis: periostitic "whiskering": ischial tuberosity, iliac crest, ischiopubic rami, greater femoral
Spine: squaring = straightened / convex anterior vertebral margins = erosive osteitis of anterior
"shiny corners" = reactive sclerosis of corners of vertebral body
diskitis = erosive abnormalities of diskovertebral junction
"diskal ballooning" = biconvex shape of intervertebral disk related to osteoporotic
marginal syndesmophyte formation (in 15%) = thin vertical radiodense spicules bridging
"bamboo spine" on AP view = undulating contour due to syndesmophytosis
ankylosis of vertebral edges / center (with bony extension through disk)
asymmetric erosions of laminar + spinous processes of lumbar spine
"dagger" sign = single radiodense line on AP view
"trolley-track" sign on AP view = central line of ossification with two lateral lines of
apophyseal + costovertebral joint ankylosis (on oblique views)
dorsal arachnoid diverticula in lumbar spine with erosion of posterior elements (Cx:
Chest: uni- / bilateral coarse upper lobe pulmonary fibrosis with upward retraction of hila
reticulonodular progressively confluent opacities in lung apices
Cardiovascular (up to 80% paralleling du: aortic valve thickening + nodularity → aortic valve insufficiency

clinical.Prognosis: 20% progress to significant disability; occasionally death from cervical spine
clinical.Rx: regular lifelong exercises + NSAID; tumor-necrosis factor (TNF)-α inhibitors
